claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about your situation.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you currently have any children living with you, or are you responsible for providing for any children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for providing for my child. I have a son named Alex who is 8 years old, but he doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have a son named Alex who is 8 years old, but he doesn't currently live with you.

To help assess your eligibility, I need to understand his living situation better:

**Who is Alex currently living with, and why doesn't he live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with his mum, my ex-partner. We separated a while back and he stayed with her, but I still support him financially and am very much involved in his life.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I can see you're actively involved in Alex's life and providing financial support, which is important context.

To properly assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to check the specific rules about this situation:

**Are you legally responsible for Alex - for example, do you have parental responsibility, or is there any formal arrangement (such as a court order or child maintenance agreement) that defines your responsibility for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm his parent - I have parental responsibility for him. There's no formal court order or anything like that, but I do contribute to his upkeep regularly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Before I can give you a final answer, let me read the official Child Benefit eligibility rules to ensure I'm applying them correctly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, and I can now see how your situation applies.

Based on the rules, since Alex lives with his mother (not with you), the key question is about your financial contributions. The rules state that you can continue to receive Child Benefit for more than 8 weeks if you contribute to your child's upkeep by the same amount or more than the Child Benefit payment.

To determine your eligibility, I need to ask:

**How much do you currently contribute financially each month or week towards Alex's upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards his accommodation)? And do you know the current Child Benefit payment rate?**

(For reference, the current Child Benefit rate is typically around £21.80 per week for the eldest child, but please let me know your contribution amount so I can assess whether it meets the threshold.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £30 a week towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes, school dinners, and bits and bobs he needs. I wasn't sure exactly what the Child Benefit rate was, so that's helpful to know.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. That's very helpful to know.

Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now make my assessment:

**You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else (in this case, Alex lives with his mother), you can continue to receive Child Benefit beyond the initial 8 weeks if you contribute to the child's upkeep by an amount equal to or greater than the Child Benefit payment. 

Your contribution of **£30 per week** towards Alex's upkeep (for clothes, school dinners, and other necessities) **exceeds the current Child Benefit rate of approximately £21.80 per week**. This means you meet the threshold for continuing to receive Child Benefit.

You have parental responsibility for Alex, you're actively supporting him financially, and your weekly contribution is substantial enough to qualify you under the rules for children living with someone else.

I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office to make or update your claim if you haven't already done so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and currently lives with his mother (not with the claimant). According to Child Benefit eligibility rules for children living with someone else, the claimant can receive Child Benefit for more than 8 weeks if they contribute to the child's upkeep by an amount equal to or greater than the Child Benefit payment. The claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes, school dinners, and other necessities), which exceeds the current Child Benefit rate of approximately £21.80 per week. The claimant has parental responsibility for Alex and is actively providing financial support. Therefore, the claimant meets all the criteria for eligibility.

Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant has parental responsibility and contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the current Child Benefit payment threshold of approximately £21.80 per week. This meets the specific eligibility criteria for Child Benefit when a child lives with someone else."
